'''Michael Slicker''' (born 16 August 1978) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], and at club level for [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], the [[Sheffield Eagles (1984)|Sheffield Eagles]], the [[Keighley Cougars]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]], as a {{rlp|pr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
Slicker signed for [[Huddersfield Giants]] in May 2001.<ref>{{cite web |title=Slicker takes Giants step |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/1325587.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=12 May 2023 |date=11 May 2001}}</ref> He was forced to retire from rugby league in 2005 due to persistent knee problems, aged 26.<ref>{{cite web |title=Knee injuries end Slicker career |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/huddersfield/4118774.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=12 May 2023 |date=22 June 2005}}</ref>

Slicker won two caps (plus one as a substitute) for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in 2001–2003 while at Huddersfield Giants.<ref>{{cite book|first1= Graham|last1= Williams|first2= Peter|last2= Lush|first3= David|last3= Farrar|title= The British Rugby League Records Book|publisher= London League|isbn= 978-1-903659-49-6|year= 2009|pages= 104–105}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Michael has 2 daughters, Megan and Brogan and a wife called Kelly. They currently live in [[Oldham]].
